name: the-new-jim-crow
description:
Michelle Alexander's The New Jim Crow — an executable toolkit for understanding mass incarceration as a racial caste system in America: its history, mechanisms, human impact, and what can be done about it.

Covers 5 use cases:
① Understanding Mass Incarceration — grasp how the War on Drugs created a racial caste system
② The Mechanics of the System — how policing, prosecution, and sentencing create racial disparities
③ The Collateral Consequences — the permanent discrimination faced by people with criminal records
④ The Human Impact — stories of individuals and communities devastated by mass incarceration
⑤ Toward Reform — strategies for meaningful change.

Trigger when users say: "Mass incarceration" "New Jim Crow" "Michelle Alexander" "Criminal justice reform" "Prison system" "War on Drugs" "Racial justice" "Policing reform" "Sentencing reform" "Felony disenfranchisement"
or mention: Michelle Alexander / New Jim Crow / mass incarceration / war on drugs / racial caste / criminal justice.

Related skills: the-coddling-of-the-american-mind, battle-for-the-american-mind, the-great-displacement, clear-thinking-book.
useCase: Helps anyone understand mass incarceration as a racial caste system, its mechanisms, and what can be done to end it.
knownRisks: This topic involves systemic racism and mass incarceration; engage with sensitivity, especially when discussing with those directly impacted.
